mappings.rationaleAI: Directly covers the nature and methods of historical writing, the central concern of this section on historiography.
Herodotus is the 'father of history' and central to A.1.a.ii on classical Greek historiography.
Thucydides is a foundational figure in Greek historiography, central to A.1.a.ii.
Section A.1.d.i covers Islamic historiography as a non-Western historiographical tradition.
Represents a specific type of historical writing (A.2), illustrating how historians approach intellectual and political history.
Represents a specific approach to historical writing (A.2), demonstrating diverse types of historiographic method.
Represents a type of historical writing (A.2), illustrating social history as a methodological approach.
Represents a specific historiographic approach (A.2), illustrating diverse ways of classifying and writing history.
Represents intellectual history as a type of historical writing (A.2) with specific methods.
Cultural history represents a type of historical writing (A.2) with distinctive methods and sources.
Economic history is a type of historical writing (A.2) employing quantitative methods.
Section B.2.b lists archaeology as an auxiliary discipline for ascertaining and interpreting historical sources.
Biblical archaeology combines archaeology (B.2.b) with textual criticism (B.2.q) as auxiliary disciplines.
Section B.2.a lists anthropology as an auxiliary discipline for historical research.

mappings.vsiAuthor: John A. Matthews, David T. Herbert
mappings.rationaleAI: Section B.2.i lists geography as an auxiliary discipline for historical research.
Section B.2.l lists linguistics as an auxiliary discipline for ascertaining and interpreting sources.
Section B.2.g lists epigraphy as an auxiliary discipline, and hieroglyphs are a primary form of ancient written sources.
Written materials (B.1) are a primary source for history, and paleography (B.2.m) studies ancient scripts.
Classical studies involve historiography of the ancient world (A.1.a) and employ many auxiliary disciplines (B.2).
Section A.1.b.ii covers Byzantine historiography as a distinct medieval tradition.
Section A.1.c.iii covers Enlightenment historiography in the 18th century.
Prehistory deals with material remains (B.1) and relies on auxiliary disciplines like archaeology (B.2.b) and dendrochronology (B.2.e).
Section B.2.o lists radiometric dating as an auxiliary discipline for historical research.
Section B.2.d lists chronology as an auxiliary discipline, and the study of time is fundamental to historiography.
Section C covers philosophy of history including speculations about the historical process and philosophical analysis.
Rousseau's philosophy of history is relevant to section C on speculative philosophy of history.
Biography represents a specific type of historical writing (A.2) with its own methods and conventions.
Autobiography is both a historical source (B.1) and a type of historical writing (A.2).
Oral history is a methodology for historical research involving interviews, archives, and narrative.
Dictionaries serve as historical sources (B.1) and connect to linguistics (B.2.l) as an auxiliary discipline.
Forensic methods relate to auxiliary disciplines for interpreting historical sources, especially material remains (B.1).
Section B.2.n lists psychoanalysis as an auxiliary discipline for historical research.
Section A.1.a.i covers Near Eastern historiography as the earliest form of historical writing.
Egyptian sources and records are among the earliest forms of historical documentation (A.1.a.i).
Landscape analysis and radiometric dating connect to auxiliary disciplines including geography (B.2.i) and dendrochronology (B.2.e).
